inputs.query: What new AI models or tools have been released recently?
inputs.context_chunks:
[Article 2] Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free. (AI | VentureBeat)
Summary: A new AI coding tool called Goose has been released, offering a free alternative to Anthropic's Claude Code, which can cost up to $200 per month. Goose can run entirely on a user's local machine, providing complete control over the AI-powered workflow and eliminating subscription fees, usage caps, and rate limits. The tool has gained popularity, with over 26,100 stars on GitHub and 362 contributors. Goose operates as a command-line tool or desktop application, performing complex development tasks such as building entire projects, writing and executing code, and debugging failures.

[Article 4] Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI (AI | VentureBeat)
Summary: Salesforce has launched a new version of Slackbot, a workplace AI assistant that can search enterprise data, draft documents, and take action on behalf of employees. The new Slackbot is powered by Anthropic's large language model, Claude, and is available to Business+ and Enterprise+ customers. Salesforce is positioning Slackbot as a 'super agent' that can coordinate with other AI agents across an organization.

[Article 58] Runway started by helping filmmakers — now it wants to beat Google at AI (AI News & Artificial Intelligence | TechCrunch)
Summary: Runway, an AI video-generation startup, aims to surpass Google in AI capabilities by focusing on video generation as a path to creating world models. The company believes its outsider status in the AI industry is an advantage. Runway initially assisted filmmakers and now seeks to expand its AI applications.

[Article 6] Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment (AI | VentureBeat)
Summary: Nous Research has released an open-source coding model called NousCoder-14B, which matches or exceeds several larger proprietary systems in competitive programming tasks. The model was trained in just four days using 48 Nvidia B200 graphics processors. This development underscores the rapid evolution of AI-assisted software development and the intense competition in this field.

[Article 33] The PR you would have opened yourself (Hugging Face - Blog)
Summary: The article discusses a new concept, but the provided content is empty, so a summary cannot be generated. However, the title suggests a potential automated system. The article is missing, so the information is limited. The title implies a system that can automate personal tasks.

output.answer: New AI models or tools that have been released recently include Goose, a free alternative to Anthropic's Claude Code, Nous Research's NousCoder-14B, an open-source coding model, and Salesforce's new Slackbot AI agent, powered by Anthropic's large language model, Claude.
